perturbation.original_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not apply to any Confidential Information that: (a) is independently developed by Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, or is lawfully received free of restriction from a third party having the right to furnish such Confidential Information; (b) has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party; (c) at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party was known to such party free of restriction; or (d) Disclosing Party agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not apply to any Confidential Information, regardless of how it was obtained. This includes, but is not limited to, information independently developed, lawfully received from a third party, publicly available information, or information the Disclosing Party agrees is free of restrictions. Even Confidential Information that was directly received from the Disclosing party will not be protected.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation fundamentally alters the exceptions to confidentiality. Originally, specific conditions (independent development, public availability, etc.) had to be met. The modified version broadens this exception to include all information, regardless of origin, even if disclosed directly by the Disclosing Party which directly contradicts the NDA's intent and other restrictions in Section 3, creating complete uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as required to accomplish the intent of this Agreement. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original. The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation. and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect to thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein. “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors and, additionally, (iii) employees of the Receiving Party and those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party, in each case, who shall be informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall agree to act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the exceptions to confidentiality, stating that the restrictions do not apply to any Confidential Information 'regardless of how it was obtained,' even if disclosed directly by the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which imposes strict obligations on the Receiving Party to keep Confidential Information confidential, not reproduce it, and only disclose it to specific representatives under specific conditions. The change essentially nullifies the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, in the event the parties have entered into, or enter into in the future, other agreements which contain terms concerning ownership or use of work product of either party or software license provisions and rights, then this Agreement shall not supersede either party’s rights and obligations as provided in such other agreements, unless such other agreement specifically provides otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall supersede any and all prior agreements, including those concerning ownership or use of work product of either party or software license provisions and rights, regardless of whether those agreements state otherwise. No other agreements are relevant, this agreement is the only valid reference.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original clause regarding the relationship of this NDA with other agreements. The original clause acknowledges and defers to other agreements regarding ownership and usage rights, whereas the modified clause asserts that this NDA takes precedence over all other agreements, regardless of their content. This will contradict Section 5 where both parties can develop, make, use, market, license or distribute products or services similar to or competitive with those of the other party disclosed in the Confidential Information as long as it shall not thereby breach this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall prohibit or restrict either party’s right to develop, make, use, market, license or distribute products or services similar to or competitive with those of the other party disclosed in the Confidential Information as long as it shall not thereby breach this Agreement. Each party acknowledges that the other may already possess or have developed products or services similar to or competitive with those of the other party disclosed in the Confidential Information. Further, either party shall be free to use for any purpose the residuals (defined below) resulting from access to or work with Confidential Information disclosed hereunder. The term “ residuals ” means information in non-tangible form which may be retained in the unaided memory by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information so long as such persons have not studied the information for the purpose of replicating the same from memory. Neither party shall have any obligation to limit or restrict the assignment of such persons or to pay royalties for any work resulting from the use of residuals. The party using any information that it claims to be residuals will have the burden of proving that the information constitutes residuals.
perturbation.justification: The changed text asserts that this agreement supersedes all prior agreements, including those concerning ownership and usage rights. This directly contradicts the original text, which acknowledges and defers to other agreements. This also conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that nothing in this agreement shall prohibit either party's right to develop competitive products or services, which is based on the understanding that pre-existing agreements concerning ownership/usage are valid. If the changed text is true and this NDA takes precedence, then the contradicted text regarding the parties' rights based on pre-existing agreements would be invalid. Therefore, a clear in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties hereto as to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder in connection with the Evaluation and merges all prior discussions between them relating thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does not constitute the entire understanding between the parties hereto as to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder in connection with the Evaluation and it doesn't merges all prior discussions between them relating thereto. Any prior conversations, emails and agreements may be relevant.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes an integration clause, stating that this agreement represents the complete understanding. The modified text negates this, suggesting that prior discussions and agreements remain relevant. This contradicts the entire purpose of the contract, whose purpose is to keep information confidential with this being the only valid agreement. This contradiction casts doubt on what exactly is binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties hereto as to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder in connection with the Evaluation and merges all prior discussions between them relating thereto. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in the event the parties have entered into, or enter into in the future, other agreements which contain terms concerning ownership or use of work product of either party or software license provisions and rights, then this Agreement shall not supersede either party’s rights and obligations as provided in such other agreements, unless such other agreement specifically provides otherwise. Neither Party will assign or transfer any rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the parties unless made in writing and signed on behalf of each of the parties by their respective duly authorized officers or representatives. Any waiver of a provision of this Agreement shall not be deemed a subsequent waiver of the same or any other provision of this Agreement. It is further understood and agreed that no failure or delay by either party hereto in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are identical, establishing an integration clause. The changed text directly negates this clause by stating the agreement does *not* constitute the entire understanding and that prior discussions may be relevant. This introduces a direct contradiction as it undermines the enforceability of the current agreement by suggesting other agreements are relevant, which creates a structural flaw, as defined in the perturbation definitions.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year beginning with the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier by either party at such party’s sole discretion upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year beginning with the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier by either party at such party's sole discretion with no need for written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The change eliminates the requirement for written notice for early termination. The original text stated that either party could terminate with 30 days' written notice, while the modified version allows termination at will, without any notice. This directly contradicts the original termination terms and creates uncertainty, contradicting Section 19. This also creates contradiction in earlier clauses that refer back to the termination section to calculate time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Information being furnished to SAP and the Company, each of SAP and the Company hereby agree that, until twelve (12) months after the date of termination of this Agreement, such party will not solicit for employment with such party or any of its subsidiaries, or employ, any of the current officers or employees of the other party with whom such party has had contact during the term of this Agreement and who became known by such party or who was identified to such party as part of the Evaluation under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing herein shall restrict or preclude either party from (A) making generalized searches for employees (by use of advertisements in the media, the engagement of search firms or otherwise), (B) continuing its ordinary course hiring practices that are not targeted specifically at employees of the other party or (C) hiring an employee of the other party who first initiates an employment discussion with such party, so long as such party has not violated the restrictions on solicitation contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text eliminates the requirement of a 30-day written notice for early termination. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that neither party can solicit employees from the other party until twelve (12) months after the termination of the agreement. Since the Changed Text allows for termination without notice, the "date of termination" becomes unclear, making the restriction in the contradicted text unenforceable or ambiguous. The original text provides clarity with a 30 day notice, but the changed text contradicts this clarity and introduces ambiguity, as per Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree the courts of the State of California shall be the exclusive venue for disputes arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that any court in the world shall be the venue for disputes arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies California courts as the exclusive venue. This change allows disputes to be heard in any court globally, contradicting the exclusive venue provision and creating uncertainty as to where legal proceedings should occur in Section 17. This would create an intext contradiction with Section 17, since it no longer has an exclusive agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, United States of America, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, other than such principles directing application of New York law. The parties agree the courts of the State of California shall be the exclusive venue for disputes arising under this Agreement. In the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be unenforceable, the remaining portions hereof shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text in the contradicted section specifies that the courts of California shall be the exclusive venue for disputes. The changed text states that any court in the world shall be the venue for disputes, which directly contradicts the exclusivity provision in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the two sections now provide conflicting information regarding where legal proceedings should occur, fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
